---
title: "opal: the open population assessment library"
date: last-modified
date-format: "DD MMMM YYYY"
author:
  - name: "N. Ducharme-Barth"
    affiliations:
      - id: pifsc
        name: "NOAA Fisheries, Pacific Islands Fisheries Science Center, 1845 Wasp Boulevard, Bldg. 176, Honolulu HI 96818"
  - name: "D. Webber"
    affiliations:
      - id: quantifish
        name: "Quantifish Limited, New Zealand"
  - name: "P. Neubauer"
    affiliations:
      - id: dragonfly
        name: "Dragonfly Data Science, Wellington, New Zealand"
  - name: "K. Kim"
    affiliations:
      - id: spc
        name: "Oceanic Fisheries Programme, Pacific Community, Noumea, New Caledonia"
  - name: "A. Magnusson"
    affiliations:
      - ref: spc
  - name: "P. Hamer"
    affiliations:
      - ref: spc
meetingtype: "SCIENTIFIC COMMITTEE"
meetingnumber: "TWENTY-SECOND REGULAR SESSION"
meetinglocation: "Apia, Samoa"
meetingdates: "11-19 August 2026"
repnumber: WCPFC-SC22-2026/SA-IP-XX
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    template-partials:
      - before-body.tex
    documentclass: SCreport
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    link-citations: true
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: SCblue
    filecolor: SCblue
    urlcolor: SCblue
    citecolor: SCblue
    toccolor: SCblue
    highlight-style: github
    code-block-bg: "#f6f8fa"
    code-block-border-left: "#c5c5c5"
    code-font: "Consolas, Monaco, 'Andale Mono', monospace"
    filters:
      - inline-code.lua
    header-includes: |
     \usepackage{draftwatermark}
     \usepackage{pdflscape}
     \providecommand{\citeproc}[2]{#2}
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    link-citations: true
    colorlinks: true
    highlight-style: github
    self-contained: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
bibliography: opal.bib
csl: apa.csl

---

```{=html}
<style>
.draft-watermark {
  position: fixed;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%) rotate(-45deg);
  font-size: 100px;
  font-weight: bold;
  color: #ccc;
  opacity: 0.5;
  z-index: -1000;
  pointer-events: none;
}
</style>
<div class="draft-watermark">DRAFT</div>
```

\newpage

# Introduction {#sec-introduction}

open, modular R package for fisheries stock assessment built on RTMB for automatic differentiation and gradient-based optimization
Builds upon the sbt R package developed by Quantifish NZ for the assessment of southern bluefin tuna by CCSBT
Draws influence from SPoRC, WHAM, Stock Synthesis & Multifan-CL

Provide a flexible modelling framework to assessment scientists capable of:
Estimating random effects
Facilitate enhanced model diagnostics
One-step-ahead (OSA) residuals
Simulation self-test
Posterior predictive checks
Directly integrating into a Bayesian estimation framework

opal  is being developed under the umbrella of WCPFC Project 123: Scoping the next generation of tuna stock assessment software
Open collaboration between SPC, IATTC, NOAA Fisheries and other interested parties



# Model structure {#sec-model-structure}

estimate age-structured population dynamics with length-based processes

Fit to multiple data types: CPUE, length composition and weight composition data

Accommodate population demographics in terms of age or length

Allows for the specification of random effects

## Population dynamics {#sec-population-dynamics}

## Likelihood components {#sec-likelihood-components}

Supports multiple likelihood options: log-normal CPUE, and multinomial, Dirichlet, and Dirichlet-multinomial length & weight composition likelihoods

# Features {#sec-features}

### Projections {#sec-projections}

Catch based population projections

### Bayesian inference {#sec-bayesian-inference}

Incorporate prior information either in a penalized maximum likelihood estimation (MLE) approach or through direct Bayesian inference via SparseNUTS

### Model diagnostics {#sec-model-diagnostics}

estimability checks, parameter correlations, simulation self-test & one-step-ahead residuals

# Case studies {#sec-case-studies}

## 2020 WCPO bigeye tuna {#sec-case-bet}

Currently implemented as age-structured production model (ASPM) with selectivity fixed and only fitting to the CPUE index

## Hawai'i 'Opakapaka {#sec-case-paka} 

Simulation example where "truth" is known
3 fisheries
Fits to two CPUE indices (with negligible observation error) & length composition data
Estimates population scale, recruitment deviations, initial age structure, and some selectivity parameters

Able to match the "true" simulated population dynamics from the Stock Synthesis operating model and producing a closer fit than the equivalent Stock Synthesis estimation model

recover the "true" parameter values within the estimated uncertainty intervals


# Discussion {#sec-discussion}

## Future development {#sec-future-development}

# Acknowledgements {#sec-acknowledgements}

Development of opal is intended to be an open and collaborative effort, inclusive to the broader fisheries assessment community. To that end we are grateful to the many people that contributed to the project through informal discussions, and participation in periodic update meetings. While not an exhaustive list, this community includes: N. Davies (Te Takina Ltd), S. Hoyle (Hoyle Consulting), M. Maunder (Inter-American Tropical Tuna Commission), R. Bi (IATTC), F. Carvalho (PIFSC), G. Pilling (SPC), J. Hampton (SPC *emeritus*), and many others including participants in the 2025 CAPAM mini workshop on developing the next generation tuna stock assessment model as well as the 2026 SPC Pre-Assessment Workshop.

# Declaration of generative AI use {#sec-ai-declaration}

Generative AI tools were used to assist with code development, refactoring, and documentation, and with drafting and editing text in this document. All analytical choices, model specifications, diagnostic interpretations, and conclusions are those of the authors, who reviewed and verified all AI-assisted output.

# References {#sec-references}

::: {#refs}
:::

\newpage

# Tables {#sec-tables}


\newpage

# Figures {#sec-figures}
